tokens: We going back to the ALSEP area.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, we'd like you to drive gingerly up to the ALSEP area there, and we're going to ask you to hold the end of that broken (heat flow) cable up in front of the TV and that will be the Station 10 parking area.
And then you can do the Station 10 tasks down to the south - correction - up to the northeast (of the ALSEP).
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: You mean out by the mortar package?!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: No, it'll be behind the mortar package.
The task will be up to the northeast.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Tony, I can't get over how hilly this place is.
It's one hill right after the other.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It sounds like an outstanding place.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well, we wish you were, too, Tony.
Tony, you're not going to see much out the right side of my in-motion Hasselblad because of this DAC camera magazine effectively blocks out that part of the field.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: As long as you have the DAC on, that sounds fine...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...Why don't you swing the DAC back and forth a little bit occasionally during the drive and get a side view.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Anybody that ever called this place "plains" - Cayley Plains - really didn't know what he was talking about.
There isn't a plain around here.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John picked the only flat place within a kilometer (of the target point) to land.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Sure glad we didn't land on any of these slopes, I'll tell you that.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Look at Hidden (Valley)...I don't know what this big crater is over here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Tony, really, the ridges here...Now, we're looking off...We're now at 007.
You just saw it in the...Well, you'll see it in the 16(-mm film).
But off to the 2 o'clock from 007 at 2.6, at north heading; there's an old subdued crater that's probably 30 meters deep.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And it didn't even show on the map!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I tell you what it did show in.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Those low angle looks (laughing) that we got at the landing site.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That fender was well needed, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Tony, we're coming in an area that's not as (pause)...not quite as blocky.
I'd say maybe 10 percent of surface is covered with cobbles (about 64 mm to 256 mm).
There's one big boulder that I just got a picture of.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: 3 meter...And it's buried from...Well, it's buried all over.
I was going to say it's mostly from wasting from upslope, but that's not true.
(Pause) And, off to our right is the big crater.
The big, deep, very subdued that just shows...I see no large rocks, no outcrops at all anywhere around there.
All I do is see big boulders that are apparently part of this ray.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm making 11 clicks (meaning km/hr), now, in this relatively smooth region.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, it's a very old surface, apparently.
Every crater here is very subdued, from the half-meter size up to the 4- or 5-meter size.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (The surface is) completely saturated (with overlapping craters).
Here come a couple of angular blocks that you just got a picture of.
They remind me of the one we sampled up there at Station 8.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're getting into an area now at 007, at 2.6 where it's more pebbly than cobbly, (the rocks on the surface) being 4 centimeters or so.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Look and see if it's between my legs.
I can't see...I think it fell between my legs.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, I didn't see it, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Wanted to know whether to go back or not.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I don't think it's worth it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And could we have a range and bearing, please?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We're at 007 at 2.6, and we can...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And that Nav system, Tony, has got us pointed right at it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: These little...Here's really a glass-covered one right there.
Little round...Looks like a bowling ball.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're doing V-max now, 8 clicks - 9 clicks; because we're going up a real steep slope.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) Okay, we just topped out on a rise, Tony; and we're going down into another swale.
I can see Flag crater off at 11 o'clock, and we're heading 007.
It (meaning Flag Crater)'s boulder strewn on the south side.
(Looking at something else) Pointed straight ahead of us, between us and the Lunar Module at...Uh-oh.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We can see the LM, though.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Now we're going down in another 2- to 300-meter - maybe 500-meter subdued crater.
That's really going to be a steep slope, if we go straight into it, but John is adroitly maneuvering around it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm not going down that critter.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Look at that hole in the bottom of it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, it's a subdued crater without any rim at all.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: But look at that; (that) hole in the bottom has a ledge in it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, this reminds me of Big Sag.
You know Big Sag on the map, west of North Ray.
Then this whole area to our right...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) I wish I could give you...(Pause) (Subvocal) I can't turn it around.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Tony, you remember those out in Hawaii...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...by time and speed, you're about 1.2 kilometers (from the LM).
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Then our distance and range is stopped.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Our distance is...Our bearing appears to be okay.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) Okay, Tony, you remember out in Hawaii, at Kapoho where we saw those very small little sink-hole craters?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This looks like a big one of those.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The one down in the middle of that hole?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: In fact, the whole area does, looks like just a big slump.
Because there's no rim to this thing at all, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It'd sure be good if you could swing the DAC over that way, if it's still running.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I can't get it over that way.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's running, but I don't have strength in my hands.
Let John turn over that way and as he swings around, I'll give you a couple of pictures of it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You should be out of film in the DAC.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Could you keep on going around?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let's just make a 360 this way.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, and get it on your Hasselblad, please.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Hey, that's a neat way to...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That would be a neat way to take a pan, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That's just what I'm doing, taking a pan of that thing.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, we got a pan from the Rover, Tony, with a 360.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And if you can turn the DAC off...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Were you able to change the settings?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well...(Pause) Apparently it wasn't running, Tony, because I've still got half a mag left.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: My arms are just too short...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...to get that thing on and off from the Rover seat.
I should have turned it on the side (before mounting the Rover at Station 9), but at 12 frames a second I thought we'd just run too much out.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I could tell you how it's doing, Charlie.
I could glance at it ever (sic) so often.
(Long Pause) (To Houston) Sure is comforting be able to hear those old wheels turning.
You can hear them; they make a rumble.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We can't hear them, but we can imagine it's comforting.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: A walking traverse in this place would be terrible.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, still in a cobbly area, Tony.
There're two...Apparently...To me there are two distinct sizes.
Those are the 6-centimeter size and below - well, around 6 centimeter - and those around 15 centimeters.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I'm V-max right now, Houston, and up slopes we're going about 8 (km/hr) and down slopes about 11.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The Nav system is gone completely, John.
That bearing's not even working, I don't think.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I don't think that bearing's working either.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What does the Nav system work off of, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's the LM up there, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah; it's straight ahead of us, but it's over that next ridge.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...Your Nav system works off Baker and Delta, so it should be on Baker, all right.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well, it's not counting either range, bearing, or distance.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Is your heading working at all?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Responding to Tony) Yeah, the heading's working.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll tell you how we'll get back, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, we got to go on top of that ridge over there, and then we'll be there.
John, see those blocks up on the top of Smoky?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: If you head for those, the LM was right in line with those from our last stop.
And I'm convinced that bearing was good from our last stop (and) we haven't changed much.
(Pause) Okay, Tony, coming up into the area now to our 3 o'clock position - correction 9 o'clock [and] we're heading 020 - it looks like another one of those old subdued, sag areas.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...And we're going to cut back on our Station 10 just a little bit here, and we'll skip that (TV) photography of the heat flow cable.
We'd like you to park halfway between ALSEP and the LM, and do a nominal station 10, except we'll drop the trench.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Don't want to do the trench.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, I bet that breaks you up.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: How do the consumables look, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're coming up on an area now, as we top a ridge, that is bouldery.
About 10 to 12 percent of area is covered with boulders greater than 50 centimeters.
And then it's cobbly, covering about 60 percent.
Looks like, apparently, a secondary around here somewhere caused all this.
But we don't see the crater.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we copy that, and you have enough consumables to go on a long time.
We just feel you've put in a good day.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well, why don't we stay out here and set a new world's outdoor record?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Ah, we don't need that; we got to leave...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: You don't want to do that, huh?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) We're going to set a new sleep record on this one.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Listens to Tony) Okay, there's the secondary, Tony.
We're coming up...On our 2 o'clock...(correcting himself) 10 o'clock position, there's about a, what, 50 meters, John, you think?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Fifty-meter crater that's a secondary; or, at least, it might be a primary, with these blocks just being out of it.
(Pause) That's probably a prim...I don't know whether that's a secondary or primary, though.
The block distribution seems to be (pause) radially equivalent (means "symmetric").
I think that was probably a primary punched into the old Cayley.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; and, Charlie, we think the DAC's out of film now, if you want to turn it off.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: What's that thing up there on the hill, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I was afraid you were going to say that.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We got to get over this ridge, John, and we'll see the old LM.
(Pause) Man, I am covered from head to foot!
(To Houston) Boy, those fenders really are useful, Tony.
This one we lost in the back has resulted in us being...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're going to have to really brush.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Charlie, you mean you guys are getting dirty?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Maybe that's how we'll get our extension.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) I think we're going to probably come out a little east of where we need, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: But if we do, we ought to cross the (outbound) tracks if we get too far east.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's exactly why I'm going this way, old buddy.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Must be a pretty steep slope here.
Man, look at those angular blocks there, would you.
(To Houston) Tony, here are 30 or 40 very angular blocks - 50 centimeters or so.
And they have the same character as the ones we sample back (along the traverse), so (it's) apparently ray material.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: A little comment about the regolith.
The regolith is texturally the same throughout.
The only difference is the difference in albedo that you can see on some of the fresh craters and also in the rays as we were going towards South Ray.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, Charlie, you can expect the feedwater tone.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, I just got a flag of some sort.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I'm going to Aux Water, On.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Houston, we just topped the ridge, and the LM is (chuckles) about 200 meters from us.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think we've had a comm drop.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we had our comm drop out here, and I understand Charlie got his water switched over?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, we tried to give you a call, but we had our comm drop out just about that time.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To John) How about swinging right and let me get a picture of that, John.
With the Rover (means the LM) and...a little bit more.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We want a nominal Station 10, so it's between the core...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: If we stop over here to the right by that...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) Okay, we've got it now, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, I was on Min (feedwater flow).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Lost under Tony) between the mortar package and the Rover (means the LM)?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: No...(Reconsidering) Yeah, that's a good place to park.
I've got to go over here and get the penetrometer in line...Yeah, that'd be good.
Mortar package and the Rover (means the LM).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We won't have any trouble navigating without that Navigation system, but it's just keeping them posted to where we are.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, that's what I think is so great.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: ...you get parked there, we'd like you to reset your Nav.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Which way we want to park on this one, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) I can't believe how hilly this place is.
There's not a flat place around!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Right, except where that LM is.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Duke : Except right there where that LM is.
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: That saved us a lot of time.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, it (meaning the Nav system)'s resetting, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Number 2 Amp Hour is reading as 110.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Battery 1 is) 65 and (Battery 2 is) 110; (battery temperatures) 110 and 120; (motor temperatures) off-scale low, off-scale low; and off-scale low, off-scale low.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The forward motors are not off-scale low; they're just coming up.
Make that the rear motors; they're about 210.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The forward motors are off-scale low.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, let me get out of this thing.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, mine is holding pressure at 3.95; I'm
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, I think I got...(Pause) Dadgummit.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's what mine is, too, Tony, about 33.
And I('ve) got 3.8, Min cooling, and I've got just a water flag.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Look at this place (probably meaning the area around his seat).
transcript.speaker: Duke
